description: A marker, map, or format is 'palethu' when touch can distinguish the feature or information it carries. Raised shape and changing texture are common means; pressure, position, or movement can do the same work. 'palo' (touch) names contact, while 'palethu' says that contact can support perception.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release them into open central 'a'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced lateral 'l', let air pass along its sides, and move into stressed true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', pass air along it, and finish with high rounded back 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The lip closure and broad vowel of opening 'pa' are shared with 'palo' (touch). 'palethu' then moves through a lateral into the fine airflow at the teeth, a change of contact that can recall a hand following texture.
usage_notes: Before a noun, 'palethu' can modify a marker, map, surface, or communication format. Noun use supplies tactility. The same object may be tactile for one task and merely textured in another.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: A carved line or woven change lets the hand follow form as deliberately as the eye follows color.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: 'palethu' treats touch as an information channel in its own right rather than a lesser copy of sight.

semantic_domains.physical: touch used as the sensory channel
